response: No, 3937 is not a prime number because it is divisible by 31 and 127 (31 × 127 = 3937).
reasoning_content: We need to determine if 3937 is a prime number. Check divisibility by small primes. 3937 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 3+9+3+7=22, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Ends with 7, not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*562=3934, remainder 3, so not divisible. 11: 11*358=3938, too high; 11*357=3927, remainder 10, so no. 13: 13*303=3939, too high; 13*302=3926, remainder 11, no. 17: 17*231=3927, remainder 10; 17*232=3944 too high. 19: 19*207=3933, remainder 4; 19*208=3952. 23: 23*171=3933, remainder 4; 23*172=3956. 29: 29*135=3915, remainder 22; 29*136=3944. 31: 31*127=3937? 31*127 = 31*100=3100, 31*27=837, total 3937 exactly! So 3937 = 31 * 127. Therefore it is composite. Answer: No.
